mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in FEDFUNDS over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate cash flows, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.4% as of Jan 2007 reflects elevated rates from prior tightening cycle
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns in periods of declining FEDFUNDS are not statistically significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising PAYEMS (employment growth) signals robust labor market demand and consumer purchasing power, which translates to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns over 60 trading days as earnings revisions propagate through analyst consensus.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 146,821k as of Jan 2007 reflects continued labor market strength
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining HOUST (housing starts) combined with rising CPIAUCSL (inflation) signals demand destruction in the housing sector and potential spillover to consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as growth expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month while CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,643k and CPIAUCSL at 272.265 as of Dec 2006 reflect housing market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of HOUST and CPIAUCSL shows Rank IC < 0.02 when tested separately from univariate models.

mechanism_narrative: Declining INDPRO (industrial production) signals weakening manufacturing activity and supply-side constraints that reduce corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices earnings expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 100.0951 as of Dec 2006 shows near-trend levels
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no significant difference between declining and non-declining INDPRO periods (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS (retail sales) combined with declining UNRATE (unemployment) signals strong consumer demand and tight labor market, which sustains corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises > 0.5% month-over-month and UNRATE declines or remains stable
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 621,340M and UNRATE at 5.4% as of Dec 2006
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between RSAFS and UNRATE shows no incremental predictive power over univariate models (R² improvement < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: FEDFUNDS crossing above 5.5% signals monetary tightening that increases corporate borrowing costs and reduces net present value of future cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity risk premiums expand.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.4% as of Jan 2007 is near the hypothesized threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid PAYEMS growth combined with rising CPIAUCSL signals wage-price spiral pressures that force the Federal Reserve to maintain or increase FEDFUNDS, constraining equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as inflation expectations rise.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.25% and CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth > 0.4%
evidence_refs: fact:Both series show moderate growth as of Jan 2007
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the joint interaction effect shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: GDPC1 growth deceleration below 2% annualized signals recession risk that compresses equity risk appetite and forward earnings estimates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate falls below 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19,358.176B as of Q3 2006 reflects solid but moderating growth
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods of sub-2% GDPC1 growth are not statistically significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
